items.title: Supreme Court Settles Circuit Split: Roundup Cannot Include Cancer Warning on Labels
items.abstract: The Environmental Protection Agency has consistently found no causal link between glyphosate-based pesticides and a heightened risk of cancer. As a result, EPA never required pesticide manufacturers to include cancer risk on glyphosate labels....By: Venable LLP

items.title: EPA’s 2026 Unified Agenda Includes PFAS Rulemakings
items.abstract: On July 3, 2026, the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) published the Trump Administration’s 2026 Unified Agenda. The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency’s (EPA) Unified Agenda includes the following rulemakings regarding per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS):...By: Berg

items.title: Definition Critical: EPA Approves Three New Pesticides that May — (or May Not) — be PFAS
items.abstract: The Environmental Protection Agency on June 30 approved three new pesticides that under certain definitions — (but not the EPA’s) — would constitute PFAS chemicals. The agency approved the pesticides’ registration under section 3(c)(5) of the Federal Insecticide, Fungicide, and R

items.title: Unpacking the Consequences of the Decision in Monsanto Company v. Durnell
items.abstract: For more than a decade, litigation over glyphosate-based herbicides has sat at the intersection of federal pesticide regulation, state tort law, scientific risk assessment, and broader debates over the role of juries in evaluating product safety....By: Bergeson & Campbell, P.

items.title: North Carolina’s Statewide PFAS Discharge and Biosolids Studies
items.abstract: North Carolina’s new Water Safety Act launches a statewide effort to identify the sources of PFAS (“forever chemicals”) discharges into surface waters from direct dischargers, indirect dischargers and biosolids land application sites....By: Williams Mullen

items.title: Down the Drain but Not Forgotten: PFAS Compliance in Wastewater
items.abstract: Per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances in wastewater are among the most significant and fastest-moving emerging environmental compliance challenges manufacturers will confront.  This article first appeared on CBIA's website, July 7, 2026....By: Shipman & Goodwin LLP

items.title: Do You Have a PFAS Lawsuit?
items.abstract: Per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) are chemicals that have been used for decades in an extremely wide range of consumer, commercial, and industrial products....By: Searcy Denney Scarola Barnhart & Shipley

items.title: PFAS Regulation and Litigation Continues to Evolve: Companies Should Plan Ahead Now
items.abstract: The PFAS regulatory and litigation environment continues to evolve. At the federal level, the United States Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) is defending its Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation, and Liability Act (CERCLA) designations for PFOA and PFOS, while

items.title: 2026 mid-year PFAS update: How federal and state regulation is shaping compliance and litigation risk
items.abstract: Federal and state regulation of per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS), as well as related policy initiatives such as Make America Healthy Again (MAHA), continue to accelerate and evolve, with key compliance deadlines approaching in mid-2026 and early 2027....By: DLA Piper
